skill.name: trello-automation
skill.description: Automate Trello board, card, checklist, comment, attachment, and member workflows through Rube MCP and Composio.
skill.summary: Manage Trello workflows through Rube MCP with guided board, card, list, member, checklist, and search operations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by markdown inline code and Trello field names. The real risk is legitimate but sensitive: the skill connects to Rube MCP and can create, update, comment on, assign, and attach content to Trello cards.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP With Trello Write Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to connect to https://rube.app/mcp and authorize Trello. It then guides state-changing Trello operations, including creating cards, moving cards, assigning members, posting comments, and adding attachments. This is expected functionality, but it exposes project data to a third-party MCP path and can modify user workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines clearly require a Rube MCP endpoint and Trello authorization, then describe write operations. The behavior is intentional automation, so the concern is permission and data exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across SKILL.md. These locations are markdown inline-code references to MCP tool names and Trello parameters, not executable shell or Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is markdown, and the flagged text is enclosed as documentation for tool names or parameters. No executable script, shell command, or Ruby runtime invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Field-Name False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged terms near description, Markdown support, IDs, and hex ID formats as weak cryptography. These are Trello API field descriptions, not hashing or encryption operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged contexts refer to Trello identifiers, card descriptions, and Markdown text. No cryptographic primitive, digest generation, or password storage logic appears in the file.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is A Workflow Note
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged storage of a returned card ID. This is a normal Trello workflow requirement for later checklist operations, not host or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line tells users to keep a Trello card ID returned by the API. It does not inspect the host, enumerate files, collect environment data, or run discovery commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires adding a third-party MCP endpoint. This is a legitimate setup step, but it creates a network dependency for Trello access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicitly present and used for the stated MCP connection. There is no evidence of a hidden endpoint or unrelated exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: State-Changing Trello Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill describes creating cards, moving cards, assigning members, posting comments, and attaching files or URLs. These actions can alter shared Trello boards.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The listed tool sequences clearly modify Trello resources. The operations match the skill purpose and do not show malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Trello work can become slow when every card update requires manual navigation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for boards, cards, checklists, members, comments, and attachments.
actual_capabilities: Connects Trello workflows through Rube MCP after user authorization.
Resolves board, list, card, and member names into Trello IDs.
Creates cards and optional checklists on selected Trello lists.
Moves cards between lists after finding target card and list IDs.
Adds comments, member assignments, and file or URL attachments.
Searches and filters cards across boards with Trello search tools.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP availability and an active Trello connection.
Cannot operate without valid Trello permissions for the target board.
Search results may lag after recent Trello changes.
Large checklist or bulk workflows may hit Trello rate limits.

use_cases.title: Create Project Intake Cards
use_cases.description: Create Trello cards with due dates, descriptions, and checklists from incoming work requests.

use_cases.title: Update Sprint Board Status
use_cases.description: Move cards between lists and assign owners after sprint planning or daily review.

use_cases.title: Find And Enrich Existing Cards
use_cases.description: Search for cards, add context comments, and attach related links or files.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Simple Card
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Trello card titled [title] on board [board] in list [list]. Add this description: [description].

prompt_templates.title: Create A Card With Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Trello card for [task] on [board]. Add a checklist with these items: [items]. Set the due date to [date].

prompt_templates.title: Move And Assign A Card
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Trello card named [card]. Move it to list [list] on board [board], then assign [member].

prompt_templates.title: Audit Board Work Items
prompt_templates.prompt: Search board [board] for open cards matching [query]. Summarize matches, confirm exact targets, then update only the cards I approve.

output_examples.input: Create a launch checklist card on the Marketing board.
output: Found the Marketing board and the Launch Planning list.
Created the card with the requested title and description.
Added checklist items for copy, design, review, and publishing.

output_examples.input: Move the API migration card to Done and assign Jordan.
output: Found the matching card and confirmed its exact title.
Moved the card to the Done list.
Assigned Jordan using the board member ID.

output_examples.input: Find open cards about invoices and add the policy link.
output: Found matching open invoice cards across the selected board.
Added the policy link as an attachment to the approved cards.
Skipped cards where the match was unclear.

best_practices: Resolve names to Trello IDs before making any update.
Confirm exact card matches before moving, assigning, or attaching content.
Use batch reads for large boards to reduce rate-limit pressure.
anti_patterns: Do not update search matches without confirming the exact card.
Do not use board URL slugs where Trello requires board IDs.
Do not attach files or URLs to shared boards without user approval.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Trello API key?
faq.answer: No. The skill expects Trello access through Rube MCP after user authorization.

faq.question: Can it create Trello cards?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide card creation after resolving the target board and list IDs.

faq.question: Can it move cards between lists?
faq.answer: Yes. It searches for the card, resolves the destination list ID, and updates the card.

faq.question: Can it assign members?
faq.answer: Yes. It can list board members and add a selected member to a card.

faq.question: Does it support attachments?
faq.answer: Yes. It can add file or URL attachments when the user approves the target card.

faq.question: What should users verify before updates?
faq.answer: Users should verify the Trello connection, target board, exact card, and requested changes.
